name: Nanoparticle Synthesis (LaMer)
description: The LaMer model explains monodisperse nanoparticle synthesis: precursor decomposition raises monomer concentration until a critical supersaturation triggers a single burst of nucleation, which drops the concentration below threshold so no new nuclei form, and the existing nuclei grow by diffusion under capping-ligand control into uniform particles.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Precursor solution"]
    N2["Reduce / decompose"]
    N3["Monomer conc rises"]
    N4{"Above critical Scrit?"}
    N5["Keep accumulating monomer"]
    N6["Burst nucleation"]
    N7["Conc drops below Scrit"]
    N8["Diffusion-limited growth"]
    N9["Cap with ligands"]
    N10["Monodisperse nanoparticles"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|accumulate| N3
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
